rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate boolean range expressions using declared helper variables with matching types.
Avoid referencing AADL properties or opaque data types in AGREE contracts unless explicitly represented.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid and use only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Assumptions and guarantees should be scoped to component types or implementations based on their binding level.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data types of referenced features.
Do not include AADL timing or unit properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use `eq` for helper variable declarations in AGREE contracts
Assignments must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables
Range assumptions should use named constants or visible feature names
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes
Do not reference AADL property names like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables
Do not convert Boolean domain values to integers in range comparisons
Do not use opaque data types in numeric range expressions
